raw_consumer_statement:
Dear Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, I am submitting this formal complaint regarding a fraud case that my credit card issuer has improperly denied, despite clear evidence that the charges were unauthorized and resulted from failures in the banks internal controls and fraud prevention procedures. 

The fraudulent activity occurred on XX/XX/XXXX for XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX for XXXX, involving transactions made without my knowledge or authorization. These charges were not made by me, and I reported them promptly after discovering them. 

The bank denied my fraud claims. However, this reasoning is incorrect and unsupported for the following reasons : First, I never activated the card. At no point did I authorize activation, use, or any transactions related to this account. I have a screenshot from XXXX  XXXX, XXXX, clearly showing that the card remained inactive in my account system. This confirms that I did not enable or authorize any use of the card. 

Second, if the card was used despite never being activated, this indicates a serious failure in the banks internal security, activation, or authorization process. A card that has not been activated should not be capable of processing any transactions. This strongly suggests either a system error, unauthorized internal activation, or a breach during the card delivery or handling process. 

Third, I provided the bank with a police report and documentation as part of my dispute, yet it was disregarded. 

Despite submitting all relevant evidence, including : Proof that the card was never activated A police report the bank continues to deny my fraud claim and hold me responsible for these unauthorized charges. I called them several times with the same answer from then. Last called was on XX/XX/XXXX and I asked for a Supervisor. They told me I will receive a call with 24-48 hours. After 5 days I continue waiting for the call. 

Under the Fair Credit Billing Act, consumers are protected against unauthorized use, especially when fraud is reported in a timely manner. The banks position that mere receipt of a card constitutes authorization is unreasonable and inconsistent with standard fraud and consumer protection practices. 

I am requesting that the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau review this case and require the bank to : 1. Conduct a proper reinvestigation of the fraud claim. 
2. Provide documented evidence supporting their denial.

3. Reverse all unauthorized charges.

4. Confirm in writing that I am not liable for these fraudulent transactions.

Thank you for your assistance and attention to this matter.

Sincerely,
